regime_badge_text: POSITIVE gamma regime – contained and dampened volatility
regime_paragraph_1: SPX spot at 7132.59 sits inside the active dealer control zone between 7125 and 7150, with net gamma exposure at +32B reinforcing a positive gamma environment. This central gamma bucket acts as a volatility damper, promoting measured mean reversion and premium decay through the session.
regime_paragraph_2: Charm is not yet influential late day and only begins to modestly support intraday drift. Since spot is not near the charm pin at 7175, charm effects favor ongoing compression and stabilization rather than strict fixation. Vanna is of moderate relevance but secondary to the dominant gamma structure and current premium levels.
regime_paragraph_3: The persistent containment between the main call barrier at 7175 and distant put floor at 6800 defines the corridor. Acceptance above 7175 would challenge this regime and require revaluation, while failure below the control zone returning beneath 7125 could degrade the current dampened setup and increase directional risk.
primary_setup_body: With spot inside the gamma-rich control zone and positive net GEX, iron condors targeting call strikes near 7150–7175 and put wings around 7100–7125 offer defined risk premium decay opportunities. The regime favors structures that benefit from containment and mean reversion within these boundaries.
secondary_setup_body: Selling call spreads just below the 7175 call barrier capitalizes on the expected resistance and the containment effect of positive gamma, especially if spot fails acceptance above this level. This aligns with the late-day charm zone centered at 7175 reinforcing call-side premium erosion.
avoid_body: Given the strong positive gamma and the spot location inside the control zone, directional debit spreads carry elevated risk as the regime promotes mean reversion and premium decay rather than trend acceleration.
wildcard_body: While currently contained, acceptance and sustained trading above 7175 would invalidate the positive gamma regime locally, increasing spot acceleration potential and necessitating a reassessment of positioning and trade flavor.

options_trade_ideas.name: Iron Condor 7100/7125 Puts and 7150/7175 Calls
options_trade_ideas.thesis: Capitalize on high positive gamma inside the control zone to harvest premium with limited risk while spot remains between 7125 and 7150.
options_trade_ideas.when_valid: While spot holds within 7125–7150 and fails acceptance above 7175.
options_trade_ideas.risk_note: Invalidated by acceptance above 7175 or breakdown below 7125.

options_trade_ideas.name: Call Credit Spread 7150–7175
options_trade_ideas.thesis: Benefit from call-side premium erosion and containment under the call wall reinforced by charm pin at 7175.
options_trade_ideas.when_valid: As long as spot remains below 7175 and does not gain sustained acceptance above.
options_trade_ideas.risk_note: Risk increases if spot accepts above 7175.

options_trade_ideas.name: Put Credit Spread 7100–7125
options_trade_ideas.thesis: Play support within the lower gamma bucket of the control zone as dealers remain long gamma and contain downside moves.
options_trade_ideas.when_valid: Valid while spot stays above 7100 and inside the control zone.
options_trade_ideas.risk_note: Fails if spot slips under 7100 with sustained acceptance.

timing_notes.note: Charm begins to exert subtle influence on drift and compression but only reinforces pinning if spot nears 7175.
Ideal timing for initiating short premium structures inside the control zone with positive gamma.
Gamma decay accelerates premium erosion as expiration approaches in this positive gamma regime.

summary_cards.pin_zone: Control zone between 7125 and 7150 dominating intraday flow
summary_cards.bull_trigger: Sustained acceptance above 7175 challenges containment, signaling bullish acceleration risk
summary_cards.bear_trigger: Breaking and sustaining below 7125 increases directional downside risk
summary_cards.wildcard: Watch for acceptance above 7175 invalidating the regime and increasing volatility
